name: Banana Meringue Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. To make pastry: In a food processor, combine flour, sugar and butter. Process until the mixture is fine meal. Add beaten egg; continue to process just until dough starts to come together.
2. Form the dough into a ball and refrigerate for 1 hour or chill in the freezer for 15 minutes.
3. Roll out the dough on a floured surface or between two sheets of plastic wrap to an 11-inch circle. Fit it into a nine-inch flan pan with removable bottom, trimming edges as needed.
4. Chill the pastry for 30 minutes and bake at 375°F (190°C) until light golden in color, about 12 minutes.
5. Thinly slice five of the bananas and arrange on the bottom of the baked pastry shell.
6. Mash the remaining bananas and combine with warm cream, 1/2 cup sugar, cornstarch, and vanilla.
7. Pour the mixture over the banana layer and bake at 350°F (175°C) for 35-40 minutes, or until the filling is set.
8. Let the pie cool slightly. To make meringue, beat egg whites with a pinch of salt until soft peaks form. Gradually add 1/3 cup sugar, beating until the meringue is stiff and glossy.
9. Spread the meringue over the warm pie and bake at 350°F (175°C) for 12-15 minutes, or until browned. Let cool before refrigerating until serving time.
